006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *

010 *

012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.svl;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
021
022import java.io.*;
023
024/**
025 * Abstract superclass of all Simple Var Language variables.
026 *
027 * <p>
028 * Vars are used to convert simple variables of the form <js>"$varName{varKey}"</js> into something else by the
029 * {@link VarResolver} class.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * Subclasses must implement one of the following two methods:
033 * <ul class='javatree'>
034 *    <li class='jm'>{@link #resolve(VarResolverSession,String)} - For simple vars.
035 *    <li class='jm'>{@link #resolveTo(VarResolverSession,Writer,String)} - For streamed vars.
036 * </ul>
037 *
038 * <p>
039 * Subclasses MUST implement a no-arg constructor so that class names can be passed to the
040 * {@link VarResolver.Builder#vars(Class...)} method.
041 * <br><b>They must also be thread safe!</b>
042 *
043 * <p>
044 * Two direct abstract subclasses are provided to differentiated between simple and streamed vars:
045 * <ul class='javatree'>
046 *    <li class='jac'>{@link SimpleVar}
047 *    <li class='jac'>{@link StreamedVar}
048 * </ul>
049 *
050 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
051 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SimpleVariableLanguageBasics">Simple Variable Language Basics</a>
052 * </ul>
053 */
054@SuppressWarnings({
055   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase naming convention
056   "java:S1452"  // Wildcard may appear in subclass method signatures
057})
058public abstract class Var {
059
060   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
061   private static final String ARG_name = "name";
062
063   private final String name;
064   final boolean streamed;
065
066   /**
067    * Constructor.
068    *
069    * @param name The name of this variable.
070    * @param streamed
071    *    Whether this variable is 'streamed', meaning the {@link #resolveTo(VarResolverSession, Writer, String)} method
072    *    is implemented.
073    *    If <jk>false</jk>, then the {@link #resolve(VarResolverSession, String)} method is implemented.
074    */
075   protected Var(String name, boolean streamed) {
076      assertArgNotNull(ARG_name, name);
077      this.name = name;
078      this.streamed = streamed;
079
080      for (var i = 0; i < name.length(); i++) {
081         // Need to make sure only ASCII characters are used.
082         var c = name.charAt(i);
083         if (c < 'A' || c > 'z' || (c > 'Z' && c < 'a'))
084            throw illegalArg("Invalid var name.  Must consist of only uppercase and lowercase ASCII letters.");
085      }
086   }
087
088   /**
089    * The interface that needs to be implemented for subclasses of {@link SimpleVar}.
090    *
091    * @param session The session object used for a single instance of a var resolution.
092    * @param arg The inside argument of the variable.
093    * @return The resolved value.
094    * @throws Exception Any exception can be thrown.
095    */
096   @SuppressWarnings({
097      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
098   })
099   public abstract String resolve(VarResolverSession session, String arg) throws Exception;
100
101   /**
102    * The interface that needs to be implemented for subclasses of {@link StreamedVar}.
103    *
104    * @param session The session object used for a single instance of a var resolution.
105    * @param w The writer to send the resolved value to.
106    * @param arg The inside argument of the variable.
107    * @throws Exception Any exception can be thrown.
108    */
109   @SuppressWarnings({
110      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
111   })
112   public abstract void resolveTo(VarResolverSession session, Writer w, String arg) throws Exception;
113
114   /**
115    * Returns whether nested variables are supported by this variable.
116    *
117    * <p>
118    * For example, in <js>"$X{$Y{xxx}}"</js>, $Y is a nested variable that will be resolved if this method returns
119    * <jk>true</jk>.
120    *
121    * <p>
122    * The default implementation of this method always returns <jk>true</jk>.
123    * Subclasses can override this method to override the default behavior.
124    *
125    * @return <jk>true</jk> if nested variables are supported by this variable.
126    */
127   protected boolean allowNested() {
128      return true;
129   }
130
131   /**
132    * Returns whether variables in the resolved contents of this variable should also be resolved.
133    *
134    * <p>
135    * For example, if <js>"$X{xxx}"</js> resolves to <js>"$Y{xxx}"</js>, then the $Y variable will be recursively
136    * resolved if this method returns <jk>true</jk>.
137    *
138    * <p>
139    * The default implementation of this method always returns <jk>true</jk>.
140    * <br>Subclasses can override this method to override the default behavior.
141    *
142    * <div class='warn'>
143    * As a general rule, variables that resolve user-entered data should not be recursively resolved as this may
144    * cause a security hole.
145    * </div>
146    *
147    * @return <jk>true</jk> if resolved variables should be recursively resolved.
148    */
149   protected boolean allowRecurse() {
150      return true;
151   }
152
153   /**
154    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this variable can be resolved in the specified session.
155    *
156    * <p>
157    * For example, some variable cannot resolve unless specific context or session objects are available.
158    *
159    * @param session The current session.
160    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this variable can be resolved in the specified session.
161    */
162   protected boolean canResolve(VarResolverSession session) {
163      return true;
164   }
165
166   /**
167    * Returns {@code true} if this variable's resolved value is stable for the lifetime of the
168    * resolver — same body produces the same output regardless of when (or in what session) it
169    * is resolved.
170    *
171    * <p>
172    * Stable vars opt in to <b>compile-time folding</b> by {@link VarTemplateCompiler}: rather
173    * than producing a {@link VarRefSegment} that dispatches at resolve time, the compiler
174    * resolves the var <i>once</i> at compile time and stores a {@link LiteralSegment} carrying
175    * the resolved value. This eliminates the per-resolve dispatch cost for hot paths like
176    * {@code @Value("${HOSTNAME:localhost}")} where the underlying var (env / system property
177    * / etc.) is treated as a build-time constant by the application.
178    *
179    * <p>
180    * Default: {@code false}. Vars that read mutable session-scoped state (request beans, args,
181    * config files) <b>must</b> leave this {@code false} — folding would cache an arbitrary
182    * resolution as a literal and break per-session correctness.
183    *
184    * <p>
185    * Common opt-ins:
186    * <ul>
187    *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.vars.SystemPropertiesVar SystemPropertiesVar} — system
188    *       properties are mutable in principle (via {@link System#setProperty}) but treated as
189    *       stable in normal application code; if a deployment relies on post-resolver mutations,
190    *       opt-out by sub-classing.
191    *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.vars.EnvVariablesVar EnvVariablesVar} — process
192    *       environment is immutable.
193    *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.vars.DotenvVar DotenvVar} /
194    *       {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.vars.EnvFileVar EnvFileVar} — .env files are read
195    *       once at startup.
196    * </ul>
197    *
198    * <h5 class='section'>Folding contract:</h5>
199    * <p>
200    * When a {@link Var} returns {@code true} here, the compiler invokes
201    * {@link #doResolve(VarResolverSession, String)} on a no-bean session at compile time.
202    * Implementations must not throw on absent session beans during stable folding; if the
203    * stable resolution would itself depend on session state (i.e. it isn't really stable),
204    * either return {@code false} from this method or throw a {@link VarResolverException}
205    * from {@link #doResolve(VarResolverSession, String)} which the compiler treats as
206    * "cannot fold; emit a normal {@link VarRefSegment}".
207    *
208    * @return {@code true} if this variable's resolution is invariant across sessions.
209    */
210   protected boolean isStable() {
211      return false;
212   }
213
214   /**
215    * The method called from {@link VarResolver}.
216    *
217    * <p>
218    * Can be overridden to intercept the request and do special handling.
219    * <br>Default implementation simply calls resolve(String).
220    *
221    * @param session The session object used for a single instance of a string resolution.
222    * @param arg The inside argument of the variable.
223    * @return The resolved value.
224    * @throws Exception Any exception can be thrown.
225    */
226   protected String doResolve(VarResolverSession session, String arg) throws Exception {
227      return resolve(session, arg);
228   }
229
230   /**
231    * Return the name of this variable.
232    *
233    * <p>
234    * For example, the system property variable returns <js>"S"</js> since the format of the variable is
235    * <js>"$S{system.property}"</js>.
236    *
237    * @return The name of this variable.
238    */
239   protected String getName() { return name; }
240}
